rationale.R1.2: A critical internal logical contradiction must be flagged: the Cramér–von Mises W² statistic measures deviation from uniformity, so a lower value indicates better PIT calibration. The baseline achieves W²=0.031 versus FlowPhot's W²=0.084, meaning the baseline is better calibrated by this metric. The artifact nonetheless asserts 'The favorable W² value for FlowPhot … confirms that the FlowPhot posteriors are closer to the ideal uniform distribution,' which inverts the direction of the evidence. Verifying evidence-claim alignment requires surfacing this logical inconsistency in how the PIT result is interpreted.
rationale.R1.3: This is the exact task specified by the delegation and is load-bearing. Responsibilities include: (a) confirming η_0.15 and σ_NMAD improvements are correctly reported and directionally support point-prediction accuracy claims; (b) checking whether these metrics, which measure point-prediction scatter and outlier rates, actually constitute evidence for interval calibration as claimed; (c) verifying the '~30% improvement' figure ((0.027−0.021)/0.027≈22% in σ_NMAD; (4.7−3.1)/4.7≈34% in η_0.15 — neither cleanly equals 30%, and the basis is unstated); (d) checking whether Table 3 supports the 'full magnitude range' scope of the central claim, given only the faint-regime bin is explicitly cited; and (e) flagging that the PIT W² values contradict rather than corroborate the calibration claim.
